starter.expected: Complete seed-by-arm matrix, paired-row, and deadline-gate checks all print true.
starter.boundary: The starter calculates a decision fixture; it does not reproduce or train the learner-selected embodied system.

buildOrder: Preregister construct, falsifier, matrix, gates, and stop rule
Reproduce baseline acceptance on every seed/cell
Restore one paired checkpoint and change one mechanism
Package a bounded claim and next falsifier

acceptanceChecks.action: Run correctness, behavior, grounding, timing, constraint, and recovery acceptance for every declared baseline seed/cell.
acceptanceChecks.expected: No intervention is interpreted until baseline discrepancies are resolved or retained as failed reproduction rows.

acceptanceChecks.action: Take the Cartesian product of declared seeds, cells, and arms and join it to raw artifacts.
acceptanceChecks.expected: Every expected key has exactly one measured, failed, or predeclared-excluded record; duplicate and missing keys block analysis.

acceptanceChecks.action: Compute paired run-level effects and uncertainty, then apply grounding, assistance, constraint, and deadline gates.
acceptanceChecks.expected: A favorable average cannot rescue a treatment with a non-compensable gate failure.

decision: The treatment has favorable success rows but is infeasible under the predeclared zero-deadline-miss gate; all four paired rows are required, so treatment-only averaging cannot support acceptance.
failure: The seed-23 treatment deadline failure is retained with its paired baseline, trajectory, checkpoint, timing stages, assistance, and budget ledger and prevents a favorable mean from becoming an accepted conclusion.
boundary: This is a deterministic decision fixture, not an authentic training run, simulator benchmark, physical experiment, safety result, or universal intervention finding; a learner study must generate and preserve its own execution evidence.
